text: **Anna:** Hi there!
**Anna:** {Washington|首都/都市|/ˈwɑːʃɪŋtən/|Washington is the capital of the USA.}, D.
**Anna:** C.
**Anna:** has some great {coffee shops|喫茶店|/ˈkɔːfi ʃɑːps/|I love coffee shops near my home.}.
**Anna:** My {favorite|お気に入り|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite book.} is this one, Busboys and Poets.
**Anna:** Actually, it's more than a coffee shop.

**Anna:** It's also a {bookstore|本屋|/ˈbʊkstɔːr/|I bought a book at the bookstore.}, a {restaurant|レストラン|/ˈrestərɑːnt/|We ate dinner at a nice restaurant.}, and a {theater|劇場|/ˈθɪətər/|We watched a play at the theater.}.
**Anna:** Marsha and I love coming here.
**Marsha:** Hey Anna, my {is having|開催する|/hæv/|We are having a party tonight.} a party on Saturday.
**Marsha:** Can you come with me?
**Anna:** Sorry, I can't come with you.
**Anna:** I have to get my {driver's license|運転免許証|/ˈdraɪvərz ˈlaɪsəns/|I got my driver's license last year.}.
**Marsha:** Will you be busy all day?
**Anna:** I don't know.
**Anna:** First, I have to take a {test|試験|/test/|I will take a test tomorrow.} on the computer.
**Anna:** Then I have to take a test in the car
**Marsha:** You have to take the test during the day, don't you?
**Anna:** Yes.
**Marsha:** The party is at night
**Anna:** Oh, then I can come with you to the party on Saturday night.
**Anna:** Great!
**Marsha:** I have to help my {friend|友人|/frend/|My friend is coming to visit.} with the party.
**Marsha:** Can you help me?
**Anna:** Sure.
**Anna:** That sounds like fun.
**Marsha:** Everyone has to bring something or do something.
**Marsha:** You can bring food or you can {perform|演じる/演奏する|/pərˈfɔːrm/|The musicians will perform tonight.}
**Anna:** Really?
**Anna:** I can perform?
**Marsha:** You can.
**Marsha:** Can you?
**Anna:** Yes, I can {recite|暗唱する/朗読する|/rɪˈsaɪt/|She can recite Shakespeare.} poetry.
**Anna:** A poem.
**Anna:** Light, dark
**Anna:** Darkness dark.
**Marsha:** In this country, nobody {recites|暗唱する/朗読する} poetry at parties.
**Marsha:** Hmm, can you do anything else?
**Anna:** Hmm, yes, I can do a {card|カード/トランプ} trick.
**Anna:** Your card is the Ten of Diamonds.
**Anna:** No?
**Anna:** Pick a card, any card.
**Anna:** Great.
**Marsha:** Anna, maybe you can just bring food.
**Anna:** No, I can't {cook|料理する/調理する|/kʊk/|I can cook pasta.}, and I really want to {perform|演じる/実演する|/pərˈfɔːrm/|He will perform on stage.}.
**Anna:** You know there is one thing I can do.
**Anna:** Trouble in mind.
**Anna:** I'm blue, but I won't be blue always
**Anna:** The sun's gonna shine in my back door someday.
**Anna:** That's it.
**Marsha:** You can sing at the party.
**Marsha:** Now I have to go {shopping|買い物する|/ˈʃɑːpɪŋ/|I go shopping every weekend.} for food.
**Marsha:** Can I help?
**Anna:** I'm not busy right now.
**Anna:** Sure.
**Marsha:** Let's go.
**Anna:** We have to go.
**Anna:** I have to help Marsha {shop|買い物する|/ʃɑːp/|I shop at the local market.}
**Anna:** And I have to practice my song.
**Anna:** Trouble in mind, I'm blue.
**Anna:** Till next time.
